title: blumea lacera var. subdivaricata
intro: Blumea lacera var. subdivaricata, also known as White Sagebrush or Small-leaved Blumea, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to Australia and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Blumea lacera var. subdivaricata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and aid in digestion. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms.
